architecture_fit.note: Smallest possible delta from base_model.py's attention block — one head, no MLP, just softmax(K·q/tau); directly answers the argmax question with 0.99 winner mass at canonical sep.

baseline_comparison.note: Explicit no-exp (relu-normalised) strawman contrasted against the softmax head plus uniform 1/N floor across the noise sweep — clean method-succeeds/strawman-fails demonstration.

faithfulness.note: Purely synthetic circuit, honestly flagged; the exp-vs-no-exp knockout (holding the rest fixed) is the causal evidence, but there is no trained model to activation-patch — inherent ceiling.

operating_range.note: Separation sweep (0–4) plus noise sweep over >2 orders of magnitude (0.1→10) that explicitly shows where the argmax property collapses; no silent failure.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely hand-set analytic circuit (tau=0.25, no learned params) that reproduces the target argmax behaviour — strong evidence of mechanism understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Side-by-side red-winner bar charts (exp vs no-exp) with live sep/noise/tau/seed sliders plus a log-x noise-sweep line plot; structurally clean, exposes demo: gr.Blocks with events inside the Blocks context.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties winner-mass on the y-axis, log noise on x, and the uniform/no-exp baselines to the goal, framed as the smallest artefact that flips the claim.

notes: A clean, minimal hand-built softmax head that directly answers the argmax question, hitting 0.99 winner mass and rank-1 at the canonical separation with near-zero entropy. It pairs an explicit no-exp strawman and a >2-order-of-magnitude noise sweep that honestly shows where the argmax behaviour breaks, and the side-by-side interactive viz makes the claim legible. The only ceiling is faithfulness: being purely synthetic, the exp/no-exp swap stands in for a true model ablation, which the README correctly acknowledges.
